result:
The plan is written to the plan file and I've signalled readiness for your review. The plan covers the full build — domain engine with the 4-strategy abstraction, all 7 programs with canon tests, persistence with history-preserving program switching, the core no-math UI loop, rest timer with backgrounded notifications, and a Live Activity scaffold — sequenced into 7 phases that each stay green on `tsc` and tests.

Whenever you're ready, approve the plan (or tell me to adjust the delivery bar / canon-source approach noted at the bottom) and I'll begin with Phase 1.

tool_input.command: ls -la && echo "---REFERENCE---" && ls -la reference 2>/dev/null && echo "---NODE---" && node --version 2>/dev/null && npx --version 2>/dev/null

tool_input.query: Expo SDK 56 release react-native version expo-sqlite expo-notifications
nSuns 531 LP spreadsheet 9 sets reps progression AMRAP rules
GZCLP T1 T2 T3 progression rules stages canonical

questions.question: The brief is explicitly larger than one pass may finish and says to prioritize the core loop. Where should I aim the deliverable?

options.label: Domain-complete + core UI
options.description: All 7 programs fully correct & unit-tested (the primary success criterion), full persistence/migrations, plus working UI for the core loop: onboarding, Today, set logging, weight selector, plate calc, rest timer. Progress/History screens present but lighter. Live Activity scaffolded. Recommended.

options.label: Broad but shallow
options.description: Attempt every screen, chart, and the Live Activity end-to-end. Risks several programs and screens being half-correct rather than a few being solid.

options.label: Narrow vertical slice
options.description: Fewer programs (e.g. 5x5, 5/3/1, GZCLP) but everything around them deeply polished and tested. Sacrifices the '7 programs' criterion for depth.

questions.question: Several programs have contested canonical definitions (especially nSuns 9-set %s and the GZCLP cascade). How should I pin them?

options.label: I pin & document
options.description: I choose one widely-cited source per program (Wendler 2nd ed, Cody Lefever's GZCLP, the original nSuns spreadsheet, Metallicadpa PPL, etc.), cite it in a source comment, and lock canon tests to it. Flag contested ones in a handoff note. Recommended.

options.label: You provide sources
options.description: You have specific spreadsheet versions / write-ups you want me to match exactly. I'll wait for links before locking the canon tests.

allowedPrompts.prompt: install packages via npx expo install and npm install
run type-check and linters (npx tsc --noEmit, eslint, prettier)

tool_input.plan:
# Compound Strength App — Implementation Plan (no-runtime variant)

## Context

We're building **v1 of a barbell-centric strength-training app** (Expo SDK 56 / React Native / TypeScript / `expo-sqlite`) from an empty directory. The product's single job is to **remove all mid-workout math**: tell a lifter what to do today, the exact working weight, exactly how to load the bar, and when to add weight — across 7 named programs — while never losing history.

**This sprint is source + tests only — NO runtime** (§7 of the brief). The deliverable is a complete, reviewable codebase as it would land in a code-review PR: domain logic, persistence, services, hooks, state, components, screens, app shell, navigation, plus Jest tests and a clean `tsc --noEmit`. The platform team builds + sim-verifies in a follow-up sprint. **Forbidden:** `expo run:*`, `prebuild`, `expo start`, Metro, `expo export`, `xcodebuild`, `pod install`, any simulator/emulator/device interaction (`simctl`, `idb`, etc.).

**Why this design:** the brief is deliberately larger than one pass can finish. The plan prioritizes the **primary success criterion** (all 7 programs prescribe + progress per canon, unit-tested) and the **core loop** UI, treats Progress/History as lighter, and scaffolds the Live Activity as the explicit best-effort bonus.

### Assumptions (defaults chosen because the clarifying questions were dismissed — flag at approval if wrong)
- **Delivery bar = Domain-complete + core UI**: all 7 programs fully correct & tested; full persistence/migrations; working code for onboarding, Today, set logging, weight selector, plate calculator, rest timer + backgrounded notification; Progress/History present but lighter; Live Activity scaffolded only.
- **Canon = I pin & document**: one widely-cited source per program, cited in a source comment, canon tests locked to it; contested ones (nSuns 9-set %s, GZCLP cascade, Madcow weekly delta) called out in the handoff note.
- **Pounds only, 45 lb bar default, standard plates**, plate inventory configurable (§10). Units field exists in the model but UI is lb-first.
- **Package versions pinned via `npx expo install`** (per §7), never hand-edited — `expo install` resolves SDK-56-compatible versions. No global Expo CLI.

---

## Architecture: the program engine (the crux)

One narrow, program-agnostic interface; **4 progression strategies** behind it (not 1, not 7 — the minimum set where each member is a genuinely different state machine). All state is JSON-serializable for SQLite; progression is a **pure function** `(def, state, result) → nextState` — the primary unit-test target.

```ts
interface ProgramEngine {
  prescribe(def, state, rotationIndex): WorkoutPrescription; // plate-rounded, concrete
  progress(def, state, result): ProgramState;                // pure
}
```

| ProgressionKind | Programs | Unique state machine |
|---|---|---|
| `linear_per_session` | 5×5, 5×3, Reddit PPL | working weight + consecutive-failure counter; bump on completion (AMRAP-gated for PPL); 10% deload at 3 fails |
| `tm_wave` | 5/3/1, nSuns | Training Max + week-in-wave; AMRAP-rep count drives TM bump only at wave boundary; `variant: 'wendler' \| 'nsuns'` selects pct tables + bump fn |
| `weekly_topset` | Madcow | weekly top set; all sets are % of it; top set climbs ~weekly |
| `tiered_cascade` | GZCLP | per-tier (T1/T2/T3) stage ladder; fail advances stage (5×3+→6×2→10×1→reset), not deload |

Parameter differences *within* a class (5×5 vs 5×3 vs PPL; Wendler vs nSuns) are **data in `SchemeSpec`/percentage tables**, not separate code paths.

### Key seams
- **Rotation is decoupled from progression** (`rotation.ts`): `Today = dayTemplates[rotationIndex % rotationLength]`. No calendar field anywhere → "missed days never skip" is automatic; nothing advances except a completion event. `inProgressSessionId` non-null ⇒ resume. Completion does `progress(...)` + `rotationIndex++` + clear-session in **one SQLite transaction** so rotation/progression never desync.
- **Plate rounding is display-only** (`plates.ts`): strategies emit *ideal* float weights; the engine rounds to loadable as the last step of `prescribe`. **Progression math uses the stored ideal weight / TM, never the rounded value** — prevents rounding error compounding across cycles. Resolver returns per-side plate breakdown for the loading UI and never suggests a plate not in inventory.
- **Onboarding seeds all 7 program states** from the user's starting numbers via `def.seedState(inputs)`, persisted up front → a later program switch just flips active `programId`; history is keyed by absolute session, untouched.
- **PR/e1RM scope:** only `role: 'main'` working sets count; warm-ups & supplemental/accessory excluded. Carried as `countsForPR`/`countsForProgression` flags on each `SetPrescription`.

---

## Module layout

```
src/domain/                      # pure, no React, no SQLite — 100% unit-testable
  ids.ts            types.ts     # ProgramId/LiftId/SlotRole/ProgressionKind; SchemeSpec, prescription/result types, ProgramState union
  engine.ts         rotation.ts  # ProgramEngine dispatch; pure rotation advance/resume
  plates.ts         e1rm.ts      # PlateResolver; Epley e1RM + PR detection
  warmup.ts                      # auto warm-up ramp to working weight (excluded from PR/progression)
  onboarding.ts                  # OnboardingInputs, "not sure" → conservative defaults, recommendation mapping
  programs/  sl5x5 lin5x3 wendler531 madcow5x5 gzclp nsuns531 redditppl + index.ts (PROGRAM_REGISTRY)
  strategies/ linearPerSession tmWave weeklyTopset tieredCascade + index.ts (STRATEGIES table)
  __tests__/  rotation plates e1rm warmup engine + programs/*.canon.test.ts + fixtures/*.json

src/db/                          # expo-sqlite persistence
  schema.sql  migrations.ts      # versioned migrations (user_version pragma)
  client.ts                      # openDatabaseSync wrapper
  repositories/  sessions exercises sets programState settings  # CRUD; program-switch-preserves-history lives here

src/services/                    # side-effecting, thin wrappers (mockable for tests)
  restTimer.ts                   # timestamp-based; survives backgrounding
  notifications.ts               # expo-notifications: schedule on rest-start, cancel on return
  haptics.ts  keepAwake.ts       # expo-haptics, expo-keep-awake wrappers

src/state/                       # app-level state (Context + reducers or Zustand)
  workoutSession.ts  settings.ts  onboarding.ts  # hydrate-on-cold-start paths

src/hooks/   useRestTimer useTodayWorkout useSetLogging useProgress
src/components/  WeightSelector PlateLoad SetRow RestTimerBar Numeral Button ...  # big targets, dark, glanceable
app/  (expo-router)  _layout onboarding/* (index)today log progress history settings
modules/live-activity/  config plugin + iOS widget  # SCAFFOLD ONLY, best-effort bonus
```

---

## Build order (phased; each phase compiles + tests green before the next)

**Phase 1 — Scaffold & config.** `npx create-expo-app` (SDK 56, TS, expo-router). `npx expo install expo-sqlite expo-haptics expo-keep-awake expo-notifications`. Configure `jest-expo`, `tsc --noEmit`, ESLint/Prettier. Commit baseline. *(No `prebuild`/`start`.)*

**Phase 2 — Domain core (highest value, pure).** `ids`/`types` → `plates` → `e1rm` → `warmup` → `rotation` → `engine` skeleton. Unit-test each as written.

**Phase 3 — The 7 programs + 4 strategies.** Implement strategies, then one `ProgramDefinition` per program with `seedState`. **Per-program table-driven canon tests** (`*.canon.test.ts`): seed → feed scripted `WorkoutResult`s (hit/miss/AMRAP counts) → assert next `ProgramState` and next `WorkoutPrescription` against a locked fixture. This is the primary deliverable — get it exhaustively right. Cite a pinned source per program; flag contested numbers.

**Phase 4 — Persistence.** Schema + versioned migrations + repositories. Test program-switch-preserves-history and resume-in-progress-session against an in-memory/Node SQLite harness where feasible (otherwise repository logic is unit-tested with the DB layer mocked).

**Phase 5 — Services + state + hooks.** Rest timer (timestamp math unit-tested), notification scheduling/cancellation, haptics/keep-awake wrappers, session state with cold-start hydration. Unit-test the pure timer math and the resume-time computation.

**Phase 6 — UI (core loop first).** Onboarding flow (Welcome→Experience→Schedule→Goal→Program fork w/ recommendation→Starting numbers via WeightSelector→Confirm) → Today → set logging (1-tap common case) → WeightSelector + PlateLoad → RestTimerBar. Then lighter Progress (e1RM trend, PR feed, volume) + History list + Settings.

**Phase 7 — Live Activity scaffold + handoff.** ActivityKit config plugin + widget stub (not wired). Write the **follow-up-sprint handoff note**: assumptions, pinned canon sources + contested flags, what's stubbed, how to native-build & sim-verify next sprint.

---

## Reuse / consistency notes
- `WeightSelector` is shared by **onboarding starting-numbers** and **in-workout logging** — build once, plate-aware, no keyboard.
- `PlateResolver.round` is the single source for every displayed weight (prescriptions, selector snaps, warm-up steps) — never round anywhere else.
- One `e1rm` module feeds Progress trend, PR detection, and "not sure?" onboarding estimates.

---

## Verification (this sprint — no runtime)
- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.
- `npm test` green; **meaningful domain coverage** — every program has a canon test; plates/e1rm/warmup/rotation/timer-math each tested incl. edge cases (deload, AMRAP boundaries, missed days, micro-plate rounding, program switch).
- Manual source review checklist: reviewer can read onboarding flow end-to-end, see working weight + per-side plates wired to the domain on Today, see the 1-tap log path, read the rest-timer timestamp math + notification schedule/cancel, and read program-switch-preserves-history in the repository layer.
- **Non-goals honored**: no auth/accounts/cloud/social/sharing/push/cardio/nutrition/multi-user/non-barbell equipment.
- **Forbidden commands not run** (no build/sim/Metro).

## Open items to confirm at approval
1. Delivery bar (assumed: domain-complete + core UI).
2. Canon sources (assumed: I pin & document; contested = nSuns %s, GZCLP cascade, Madcow weekly delta).
3. Pounds-only UI (model keeps a unit field for the Stretch kg path).
